msgs.text: This proof gives presentation for the absolute Galois group of the field of 2-adic numbers as a profinite group. Is it correct?

**Not as written.** I found no simple counterexample to the proposed presentation, and several substantial consistency checks work. But the manuscript does not yet supply a complete proof of its main theorem. 

The principal gap is in Section 6. In Lemma 6.3, two choices of equivariant correction terms \(m_h\) for the same fibre quadratic form can differ by a cocycle
\[
h\longmapsto m'_h-m_h\in Z^1(H,V^\vee).
\]
Consequently, the resulting central extension classes can differ by a filtration-one class \(\Gamma_\gamma\), not automatically by a coboundary. The assertion that “naturality” makes this class vanish is not a valid general argument. In the particular modules considered here, the conclusion may be repairable by explicitly proving \(H^1(H,V^\vee)=0\)—using Maschke in the unramified case and the later projectivity lemma in the ramified case—but that missing argument is essential to the claimed independence of the base quadratic obstruction. 

A second load-bearing omission is the “half-orbit” construction in Lemmas 6.2 and 6.11. The manuscript invokes an equivariant transfer and then identifies its pullback under Shapiro with an Evens norm, but it does not actually construct the normalized cocycle or verify that identification. The local determinant sign in Proposition 6.15 depends on precisely this calculation, and that sign is subsequently used in the Fourier–Gauss induction.  

There are similar, though probably repairable, compressed steps in Lemma 8.6: the claim that a nonzero radical edge cuts every lift torsor exactly in half needs an explicit obstruction calculation, not just an appeal to perfect duality. 

On the positive side, the tame quotient, marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient, Demushkin normalization and scalar duality calculations appear coherent. I also independently checked the relation in \(A_4\) and \(S_4\): it gives respectively \(24\) and \(72\) surjections, hence \(1\) and \(3\) extensions after dividing by automorphisms, agreeing with the known \(\mathbf Q_2\) counts. Those are meaningful sanity checks, but not a proof of equality for all finite quotients. The published formulas indeed give \(1\) \(A_4\)-extension and \(3\) \(S_4\)-extensions of \(\mathbf Q_2\). 

The manuscript also says that its separate computational verification is not reproduced, so that computation cannot fill these gaps.  The problem is still publicly listed as unsolved as of June 24, 2026, although that status by itself does not disprove the proposed result. 

**Verdict:** the displayed presentation may be correct, but Theorem 1.2 is not established by the submitted proof in its present form. It should be treated as an unverified research draft pending complete cochain-level proofs of the Section 6 constructions and the corresponding induction steps.

Ok, please let us know what all to insert/modify in the proof, giving the exact contents of said insertions/modifications, in order to satisfy your critiques completely and make the proof complete and coorrect.

I prepared exact replacements and insertions for the current Lemmas 6.2, 6.3, 6.11–6.12, and 8.6. 

- [Unified patch with every exact change](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_proof_revision.patch)
- [Revised complete TeX source](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_presentation_revised.tex)
- [Compiled revised PDF](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_presentation_revised.pdf)
- [Brief revision map](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_revision_readme.md)

The revision explicitly constructs the half-orbit cocycle and its Evens-norm/Shapiro identification, proves the determinant-data independence using \(H^1(H,V^\vee)=0\), and supplies the cochain-level torsor-halving calculation for Lemma 8.6. The TeX compiles cleanly with resolved references.

These amendments close the three specific gaps I previously identified; they are not, by themselves, an independent re-audit of every other argument in the manuscript.
